metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in AC1 that `validate_move_registry` enforces `{subject}` template presence / `name`/`template` types only — applies_when vocabulary enforcement is provided by `test_move_catalog_validates_and_uses_closed_applies_when_vocabulary`; the new unit test should explicitly assert both new moves' `applies_when` tags are in `OVERLAY_IDS ∪ {'always'}`.
findings.finding: AC1 asserts that the two new moves 'pass `validate_move_registry` (built-in, strict)' as the enforcement mechanism for `applies_when` vocabulary. Reading `review_kernel/coach.py:84-87`, `validate_move_registry` only checks that `applies_when` is a list of strings — it does NOT validate tag values against `OVERLAY_IDS ∪ {'always'}`. The vocabulary enforcement lives exclusively in the existing unit test `test_move_catalog_validates_and_uses_closed_applies_when_vocabulary` (lines 78-84 of `test_code_review_ws2.py`). AC1 therefore mis-attributes the enforcement: an implementer relying on `validate_move_registry` alone could ship a `delete-bad-test` with `applies_when: ['change-detector']` and it would pass the validator but never fire.
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC1 to: '...pass `validate_move_registry` (which validates `{subject}` template and name/type) AND the existing `test_move_catalog_validates_and_uses_closed_applies_when_vocabulary` test, which enforces that all `applies_when` tags are members of `OVERLAY_IDS ∪ {"always"}`.' Remove the implication that the validator alone enforces tag vocabulary.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in AC3 that the unit test pins *presence* of delete-bad-test in the applicable set (which is what the mechanism guarantees), and either (a) accept that LLM selection is a prompt/name-quality concern outside the unit test, documenting this explicitly, or (b) add a coach prompt test that asserts the LLM actually picks delete-bad-test over add-regression-test for a change-detector subject (e.g. a golden-path integration test).
findings.finding: AC3's unit test can only assert that delete-bad-test IS in the applicable_moves set for a tests-tagged finding — it cannot pin that the LLM selects delete-bad-test OVER add-regression-test, which is always offered (add-regression-test has applies_when=["tests","always"], so ALWAYS in aw makes it unconditionally applicable regardless of triggers). The plan's stated goal ('the LLM selects delete — not improve the test — for a change-detector subject') is an unverifiable LLM-behavior claim that the pinned unit test does not confirm, leaving the key behavioral distinction unguarded.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a co-located decision rule in the coach prompt (`code-review-coach.md`) or in the move's `name`/`template` wording that disambiguates when to pick `delete-bad-test` vs. `add-regression-test` for a `tests`-tagged finding (e.g., move name 'delete the change-detector test', or a prompt note: 'for a tautological/change-detector test, pick delete-bad-test; for a missing-coverage gap, pick add-regression-test').
findings.finding: The plan asserts that for a change-detector finding, the LLM 'selects delete — not improve the test', but no decision rule, prompt instruction, or schema constraint enforces this preference. Both `delete-bad-test` (proposed, applies_when: ['tests']) and `add-regression-test` (existing, applies_when: ['tests', 'always']) will appear in the applicable subset for any `tests`-tagged finding. The coach prompt (code-review-coach.md) says only 'select the single most useful MOVE' — no tiebreaker for change-detector vs. regression-gap patterns is specified anywhere co-located with the schema or prompt.
findings.suggested_fix: In `delete-bad-test`'s entry, set `"name": "delete the change-detector / tautological test"` (making it self-distinguishing in the coach listing). Optionally add one sentence to `code-review-coach.md`: 'For a tautological or change-detector test (one that merely mirrors source text), pick `delete-bad-test`; for a genuine coverage gap, pick `add-regression-test`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit AC unit-test assertion that `file-follow-on-ticket` appears in `applicable_moves` for a finding tagged with a non-`tests` overlay criterion (e.g., `criteria: ["security"]`), symmetrically with the `delete-bad-test` AC test.
findings.finding: The Acceptance Criteria include a unit test pinning `delete-bad-test` applicability for a `criteria: ["tests"]` finding, but no symmetric unit test is stated for `file-follow-on-ticket` being offered under `applies_when: ["always"]` — i.e., that it appears in `applicable_moves` regardless of which overlay criterion the surviving finding carries.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a bullet: 'For a surviving finding tagged `criteria: ["security"]` (or any non-tests overlay), `file-follow-on-ticket` is among the applicable moves; a unit test using `review_kernel.applicable_moves` pins this.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC and unit test that asserts `file-follow-on-ticket` appears in `applicable_moves` for a finding tagged with a non-`tests` criterion (e.g. `criteria: ["security"]`), pinning the `applies_when: ["always"]` semantics.
findings.finding: The unit test plan covers `delete-bad-test` being applicable for a `tests`-tagged finding, but no AC or test asserts that `file-follow-on-ticket` is offered for a finding tagged with a non-`tests` criterion (e.g. `["security"]` or `["style"]`), which is the defining behavior of its `applies_when: ["always"]` setting. This new behavior — the move fires regardless of overlay — is introduced but not pinned by any acceptance criterion.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — third bullet; 'applies_when gating' section for `file-follow-on-ticket`
findings.suggested_fix: Add a fourth AC: 'For a surviving finding tagged `criteria: ["security"]` (or any non-`tests` overlay), `file-follow-on-ticket` is among the applicable moves (the `always` gating is exercised); a unit test pins this.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
